workflow: 💡 `Shift+Tab` cycles modes: normal → plan → accept edits. Double-tap for plan mode.
💡 `Tab` toggles extended thinking mid-conversation — no restart needed.
💡 `Ctrl+G` opens your prompt in an external editor (e.g. VS Code) for long inputs.
💡 In plan mode, `Ctrl+G` to edit Claude's plan in your IDE — refine, then send back.
💡 `/rewind` (Esc Esc) undoes off-track runs — cheaper than correcting.
💡 Commit at least once per hour; stale work is lost work.
💡 After two failed corrections, `/clear` and write a better prompt.
💡 `/compact` Focus on X — preserves only relevant context during compaction.
💡 `/btw` for quick questions — answer never enters conversation history.
💡 Start a fresh session per task — long sessions degrade quality measurably.
💡 Break tasks into '30 min of human coding' chunks max.
💡 Describe symptoms, not solutions, for bug fixes.
💡 Fan out migrations: task list → loop `claude -p` 'migrate $file'.
💡 `Esc` stops Claude mid-action — context preserved for course-correction.
💡 `claude --continue` resumes last session; `--resume` picks from recent.
💡 `/rename` sessions by task — treat them like branches, separate contexts.
💡 Interview pattern: have Claude ask YOU questions before big features.
💡 Pipe data: `cat error.log | claude` for quick analysis, no copy-paste.
💡 Use `@path` in prompts to reference files — faster than describing where.
💡 `--allowedTools` restricts permissions for unattended `claude -p` runs.
💡 `/clear` between unrelated tasks — don't pollute context across topics.
💡 Type `y` or `n` for yes/no — Claude gets it, no need to spell it out.
💡 Paste the bug, say 'fix' — don't micromanage the approach.
💡 If you do it >1x/day, make it a /command or skill.
💡 Run 3-5 Claudes in parallel via worktrees — biggest productivity unlock.
💡 /loop for recurring tasks — poll deploys, babysit PRs (up to 3 days).
💡 'Choose an approach and commit' — avoid analysis paralysis loops.
💡 Say 'change X' not 'can you suggest changes' — be action-explicit.
context: 💡 Keep `CLAUDE.md` under 200 lines; overflow → `.claude/rules/` files.
💡 Run `/compact` manually at ~50% context — never wait for auto-compaction.
💡 Context rot starts at 50-60% of the nominal max, not the hard limit.
💡 `CLAUDE.md` survives `/compact` — instructions given only in chat do not.
💡 Use @path imports for persistent context that survives `/clear`.
💡 ASCII architecture diagrams in context improve spatial reasoning over prose.
💡 Every line in `CLAUDE.md` must earn its place: would the agent fail without it?
💡 todo.md / plan.md survive `/compact` — use as attention anchors.
💡 `.claudeignore` excludes build artifacts; cuts irrelevant context from reads.
💡 MCP servers load tool defs upfront — fewer tools = lower token cost.
💡 Path-scoped rules in `.claude/rules/` load only when matching files opened.
💡 Run `/init` to scaffold a starter `CLAUDE.md` from your project structure.
💡 4 layers: CLAUDE.md (rules) → Skills (knowledge) → Hooks (gates) → Agents (workers).
💡 Use IMPORTANT/MUST for critical rules — emphasis improves model adherence.
💡 Check `CLAUDE.md` into git — it compounds in value as your team contributes.
💡 Over-specified CLAUDE.md? Convert rigid rules into hooks for guarantees.
💡 `CLAUDE.md` can @-import files — keep root short, detail in @docs/.
💡 After corrections: 'Update CLAUDE.md so you don't repeat this.'
💡 Tag @claude on PRs to auto-evolve CLAUDE.md over time.
💡 Finish migrations — partial frameworks confuse model pattern matching.
💡 'Run the tests' should work first try — fix CLAUDE.md if not.
💡 Long context? Data at top, query at bottom — up to 30% boost.
💡 Ground long-doc answers in quotes first — cuts through noise.
💡 Restrict to provided docs: 'only use info from these sources'.
agents: 💡 Pass file paths to agents, not content — they read files themselves.
💡 Subagents run in isolated context — delegate exploration to them.
💡 Feature-specific agents with scoped skills beat general-purpose roles.
💡 Subagents cannot spawn subagents — chain them from the main conversation.
💡 Set `memory: project` in agent frontmatter for cross-session knowledge.
💡 Use `isolation: worktree` to give a subagent an isolated git copy.
💡 Don't spawn a subagent for grep in 2-3 known files — Read is faster.
💡 Supervise auth, payment, security, data mutations (1.5-2x AI bugs).
💡 Agents self-report 73% success at 35% actual — always verify with tools.
💡 Different agent reviews than writes — self-review degrades quality.
💡 3 agent failures → stop and escalate. Hard caps prevent infinite loops.
💡 Fan-out agents need non-overlapping scopes or they duplicate work.
💡 Gate each pipeline step — errors propagate without verification gates.
💡 Same agent wrote tests + code? Separate them — that's why tests lie.
💡 Adversarial pattern: defender ‖ attacker → judge for design decisions.
💡 Restrict agent tools to scope: Read-only for research, Write for code.
💡 Say 'use subagents' to throw more compute at any hard problem.
💡 Test time compute: separate contexts catch bugs one agent can't see.
💡 Curb Opus subagent overuse: 'work directly for simple tasks'.
hooks: 💡 `exit 2` from a hook feeds stderr to Claude — use for corrections.
💡 PreToolUse can modify tool input (e.g., sanitize command) before execution.
💡 `statusMessage` field shows custom spinner text while a hook runs.
💡 `spinnerTipsOverride` in `settings.json` replaces default tips with yours.
💡 `async: true` on a hook runs it in background without blocking the session.
💡 `once: true` (skills only) runs a hook once per session then removes it.
💡 Keep sync hooks under 1 second; anything longer should be async.
💡 PostToolUse on `Write|Edit` auto-runs formatter — beats CLAUDE.md.
💡 Stop hook: nudge Claude to verify, keep going, or block until tests pass.
💡 Hooks are deterministic; CLAUDE.md is advisory — use hooks for guarantees.
💡 Route permission requests to Opus via hook — auto-approve safe ones.
💡 Deny `Read:*.env` and `Bash:sudo*` in permissions — safety baseline.
quality: 💡 Share screenshots of visual bugs — Claude groks images faster than prose.
💡 Tests, screenshots, or expected outputs — highest-leverage addition.
💡 Use 'ultrathink' for hard problems — surfaces chain-of-thought reasoning.
💡 Second session as reviewer — fresh context catches author bias.
💡 Reference specific files + patterns: 'Follow Widget.cs as the template.'
💡 Ask Claude to write tests first — exposes gaps before implementation.
💡 Explain-back: ask Claude to walk through its own code step-by-step.
💡 Vague prompts are valid for exploration; precise prompts are for execution.
💡 Review specs, not code — human leverage is defining WHAT, not HOW.
💡 Examples teach agents format better than rules — show, don't prescribe.
💡 Start CLAUDE.md empty; add rules only after observed agent failures.
💡 One noisy CLAUDE.md line tanks accuracy — 'just in case' = poison.
💡 Say 'undo that' to revert Claude's last changes without losing context.
💡 Read git history for context: 'why does X API work this way?'
💡 'Grill me on these changes — no PR until I pass your test.'
💡 'Scrap this — implement the elegant solution you now see.'
💡 Give Claude a way to verify its work — 2-3x quality boost.
💡 Write detailed specs — specificity is the #1 output quality lever.
💡 Prototype > PRD — build 20-30 versions, shots are cheap.
💡 Use Explanatory output style when onboarding to new codebases.
💡 Agentic search (glob + grep) beats RAG — no sync drift.
💡 Skill description is a trigger, not a summary — write for the model.
💡 Tell Claude what TO do, not what NOT to do — positive framing steers.
💡 Add WHY behind instructions — Claude generalizes from motivation.
💡 'Investigate before answering' blocks hallucination on unread code.
💡 Self-correct loop: generate → review vs criteria → refine.
💡 Match prompt formatting to the output format you want back.
💡 Don't let test cases define the solution — insist on general logic.
💡 'Go beyond the basics' — explicit ambition unlocks richer output.
💡 Let Claude say 'I don't know' — reduces hallucinated answers.
performance: 💡 Use `/model` to downgrade for simple mechanical tasks — save tokens.
💡 50k-token overhead per `--print` call; prefer SDK for tight loops.
💡 Parallel worktrees + agents cut wall-clock time on multi-file refactors.
💡 Organize skills in subfolders — flat dirs cause context bloat.
💡 `effortLevel`: low in `settings.json` reduces token cost for routine tasks.
💡 Git worktrees enable parallel branch work without context confusion.
💡 Avoid MCP servers that mirror entire APIs — prefer few high-level tools.
💡 `/permissions` with wildcards (Bash(npm run *)) — not --dangerously-skip.
💡 Install `gh` CLI for GitHub workflows — most context-efficient integration.
💡 `/plugin` to browse marketplace — bundles of skills, hooks, and tools.
💡 `/setup refresh` after adding commands/skills — surfaces them as spinner tips.
💡 `/setup customize` to list all tips by category or add your own.
💡 `/setup customize` to add custom tips — they persist across refreshes.
💡 `/doctor` checks your Claude Code installation health — run after upgrades.
💡 Code intelligence plugins add precise symbol nav for typed languages.
💡 `claude -p --output-format stream-json` pipes structured output to tools.
💡 Voice dictation: speak 3x faster than typing, richer prompts.
💡 `/sandbox` — 84% fewer permission prompts (Anthropic internal data).
💡 Use Opus with thinking for everything — less steering = net faster.
💡 CI: `-p --max-turns 3 --max-budget-usd 1.50` caps runaway agents.
